description_long: This is a dark, medieval-style helmet constructed primarily from metal, featuring a rounded dome and a pronounced, angular face guard. The helmet is adorned with a series of small, evenly spaced rivets along the lower edge and around the face opening, adding both structural integrity and decorative detail. The lower edge and the face guard are bordered with a metallic gold trim, providing a striking contrast to the matte black finish of the main body. The helmet has a segmented construction, with a separate neck guard that flares outward to protect the wearer’s throat and neck. There are no visible ventilation holes or modern features, emphasizing its historical design. The interior appears hollow, designed to fit over a human head, and the overall build suggests it is intended for ceremonial or protective use.
description: A black, medieval-style metal helmet with gold trim, rivet detailing, and a pronounced face and neck guard. The design is solid and ornate, with no visible modern features.
description_view_0: Front and side view showing the open face guard, gold trim, and rivet details along the edges.
description_view_1: Rear and side view highlighting the rounded dome, flared neck guard, and absence of any rear openings.
description_view_2: Angled front view emphasizing the symmetry of the face guard and the decorative rivets.
description_view_3: Side and rear view displaying the helmet's curvature, gold trim, and the segmented construction of the neck guard.
